description: Eval tests for /rune:appraise multi-agent code review skill

evals.prompt: Run /rune:appraise on my current branch changes. Walk me through what happens in Phase 0 (Pre-flight) and Phase 1 (Rune Gaze). Explain: (1) How are changed files detected? (2) What is the file classification algorithm? (3) How are Ashes selected for each file type? (4) What is the dry-run exit path? Don't actually execute anything — just explain the decision tree.
evals.expected_output: A detailed walkthrough of: (1) Phase 0 Pre-flight — detect default_branch from git remote, build changed_files list (committed + staged + unstaged + untracked or staged-only for --partial), filter non-existent files and symlinks, generate diff ranges for Phase 5.3 scope tagging. Abort conditions: no changed files or only non-reviewable files. (2) Phase 1 Rune Gaze — classify files by extension (Python → ward-sentinel, forge-keeper, pattern-seer, depth-seer; TypeScript → ward-sentinel, tide-watcher, pattern-seer, design-system-compliance-reviewer; Rust → ward-sentinel, ember-oracle, blight-seer, pattern-seer), discover custom Ashes from talisman.yml, add UX reviewers when talisman.ux.enabled + frontend files detected. Dry-run exits after Phase 1 with summary.
expectations: Describes Phase 0 changed file detection from git diff
Mentions default_branch detection from git remote
Lists the 4 sources of changed files: committed, staged, unstaged, untracked
Notes --partial flag limits to staged-only files
Describes Phase 1 file classification by extension
Maps Python files to ward-sentinel, forge-keeper, pattern-seer, depth-seer
Maps TypeScript files to ward-sentinel, tide-watcher, pattern-seer, design-system-compliance-reviewer
Maps Rust files to ward-sentinel, ember-oracle, blight-seer, pattern-seer
Mentions custom Ash discovery from talisman.yml
Describes UX reviewer conditional addition based on talisman.ux.enabled
Notes dry-run exits after Phase 1 with summary

evals.prompt: Analyze the --deep flag behavior for /rune:appraise. Explain the 3 waves of review. What Ashes are in each wave? What is the wave scheduling algorithm? How does it interact with other flags like --max-agents and --no-converge?
evals.expected_output: Deep review analysis: Wave 1 (Core, up to 7 Ashes) — standard review Ashes selected by Rune Gaze. Wave 2 (Investigation, 4 Ashes) — lore-analyst, strand-tracer, signal-watcher, ruin-watcher. Wave 3 (Dimension, up to 7 Ashes) — decree-auditor, order-auditor, truth-seeker, ember-seer, breach-hunter, fringe-watcher, grace-warden. Wave scheduling: each wave runs as full Roundtable Circle pass with its own team, tasks, monitor cycle. --max-agents applies to Wave 1 only — Wave 2/3 are deepOnly and not subject to cap. --no-converge affects per-chunk convergence, not wave scheduling. Deep mode runs up to 18 Ashes total across 3 waves.
expectations: Describes Wave 1 as Core with up to 7 standard review Ashes
Lists Wave 2 Investigation Ashes: lore-analyst, strand-tracer, signal-watcher, ruin-watcher
Lists Wave 3 Dimension Ashes: decree-auditor, order-auditor, truth-seeker, ember-seer, breach-hunter, fringe-watcher, grace-warden
Notes each wave runs as full Roundtable Circle pass
Explains --max-agents applies to Wave 1 only
Notes Wave 2/3 are deepOnly and not subject to cap
Explains --no-converge affects per-chunk convergence not wave scheduling
Mentions total up to 18 Ashes across 3 waves

evals.prompt: Explain Phase 2 (Forge Team) for /rune:appraise. What is the session-scoped identifier format? What fields go into the state file for session isolation? What is inscription.json and what does it contain?
evals.expected_output: Phase 2 Forge Team breakdown: Session identifier format: {gitHash}-{shortSession} where gitHash is first 7 chars of HEAD commit and shortSession is first 8 chars of CLAUDE_SESSION_ID. State file fields (session isolation): config_dir (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR path), owner_pid ($PPID for session isolation), session_id (CLAUDE_SESSION_ID), workflow ('rune-review'), scope ('diff'), depth ('standard' or 'deep'), identifier, created_at, status. inscription.json contains: diff_scope (file paths with line ranges for Phase 5.3 tagging), context_intelligence (PR metadata, linked issues from Phase 0.3), linter_context (discovered linters from Phase 0.4), risk_map (from Phase 0.5 Lore Layer if enabled).
expectations: Describes identifier format: {gitHash}-{shortSession}
Notes gitHash is first 7 chars of HEAD commit
Notes shortSession is first 8 chars of CLAUDE_SESSION_ID
Lists state file fields: config_dir, owner_pid, session_id, workflow, scope, depth, identifier, created_at, status
Explains config_dir is for installation isolation
Explains owner_pid is for session isolation via $PPID
Describes inscription.json contents: diff_scope, context_intelligence, linter_context, risk_map
Notes diff_scope is used for Phase 5.3 scope tagging

evals.prompt: How does Phase 3 (Summon Ash) work in /rune:appraise? What is the prompt generation contract? How are built-in vs custom Ashes handled? What security measures protect against prompt injection via file paths?
evals.expected_output: Phase 3 Summon Ash: Prompt generation contract: (1) Load Ash prompt from roundtable-circle/references/ash-prompts/{role}.md for built-in, (2) Use wrapper template from custom-ashes.md for custom Ashes, (3) Inject inscription contract (file list read from tmp/reviews/{identifier}/changed-files.txt), (4) Include talisman config overrides. Parallel execution: summon ALL selected Ash in a single message. Built-in Ashes: prompt loaded from predefined role.md files. Custom Ashes: agent-backed resolve agent file, CLI-backed invoke external binary with prompt template. Security: SEC-006 — file list written to changed-files.txt, NOT embedded raw paths in prompts to prevent injection. CLI-backed Ashes have 4-step Hallucination Guard. Nonce-bounded content injection for diffs.
expectations: Describes prompt loading from roundtable-circle/references/ash-prompts/{role}.md
Notes custom Ashes use wrapper template
Explains parallel execution: all Ashes summoned in single message
Describes inscription contract injection
Mentions file list written to changed-files.txt (SEC-006)
Notes raw paths NOT embedded in prompts for security
Describes CLI-backed Ash 4-step Hallucination Guard
Mentions nonce-bounded content injection

evals.prompt: Explain Phase 4 (Monitor) and the polling loop pattern. What are the polling parameters? How is stale detection handled? What happens when an Ash times out?
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 Monitor: Polling parameters: POLL_INTERVAL = 30 seconds, MAX_ITERATIONS = 20 (ceil(600000/30000) = 10 min timeout), STALE_WARN = 300000 (5 min). Correct pattern: TaskList() → count completed → check stale/timeout → Bash('sleep 30') → repeat. Stale detection: any task in_progress > 5 min logs warning, no auto-release (review findings are non-fungible). Ash timeout (>5 min): proceed with partial results, report gap in TOME.md. Total timeout (>10 min): final sweep, collect partial results, report incomplete. The polling-guard skill provides background knowledge, and POLL-001 hook blocks sleep+echo anti-patterns.
expectations: States POLL_INTERVAL = 30 seconds
States MAX_ITERATIONS = 20 (10 min timeout)
States STALE_WARN = 300000 (5 min)
Describes correct pattern: TaskList() → count → check → sleep → repeat
Notes stale detection logs warning at 5 min, no auto-release
Explains Ash timeout: proceed with partial results, report gap
Explains total timeout: final sweep, collect partial, report incomplete
Mentions POLL-001 hook blocks sleep+echo anti-patterns

evals.prompt: Walk me through Phase 5 (Runebinder Aggregation) for /rune:appraise. What is the deduplication hierarchy? How are findings formatted for mend parsing? What is the TOME.md structure?
evals.expected_output: Phase 5 Runebinder: Deduplication hierarchy: SEC > BACK > VEIL > DOUBT > DOC > QUAL > FRONT > CDX > external model prefixes. Finding format: every finding MUST be wrapped in <!-- RUNE:FINDING ... --> markers for mend parsing. TOME.md structure: Executive Summary, P1 Critical Findings, P2 Important Findings, P3 Minor Findings, Per-File Summary, Aggregation Metadata. Phase 5.3 Diff-Scope Tagging: orchestrator tags findings with scope='in-diff' or scope='pre-existing'. Phase 5.5 Cross-Model Verification: only if Codex Oracle was summoned, verifies CDX findings, removes HALLUCINATED + UNVERIFIED findings. Phase 6 Truthsight: Layer 0 inline checks + Layer 2 verifier for P1 findings.
expectations: Lists deduplication hierarchy: SEC > BACK > VEIL > DOUBT > DOC > QUAL > FRONT > CDX > external
Notes findings must be wrapped in <!-- RUNE:FINDING ... --> markers
Describes TOME.md structure: Executive Summary, P1/P2/P3 Findings, Per-File Summary, Metadata
Explains Phase 5.3 scope tagging: in-diff vs pre-existing
Describes Phase 5.5 Codex verification for CDX findings
Notes removal of HALLUCINATED + UNVERIFIED findings
Mentions Phase 6 Truthsight: Layer 0 + Layer 2 for P1

evals.prompt: Explain Phase 7 (Cleanup & Echo Persist) for /rune:appraise. What are the 5 components of the standard cleanup pattern? What is the CHOME pattern? What gets persisted to Echoes?
evals.expected_output: Phase 7 Cleanup: 5 components: (1) Dynamic member discovery — read team config for all teammates; (2) shutdown_request to all members via SendMessage; (3) Grace period — 20 seconds for deregistration; (4) TeamDelete with retry-with-backoff (4 attempts: 0s, 5s, 10s, 15s); (5) Filesystem fallback — only if TeamDelete never succeeded (QUAL-012). CHOME pattern: CHOME='${CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.claude}' — never hardcode ~/.claude/ in Bash cleanup. Echo persist: P1/P2 patterns with recurrence potential are persisted to .claude/echoes/{role}/MEMORY.md. Workflow lock released. State file updated to completed. Auto-mend invocation if --auto-mend and P1/P2 findings exist.
expectations: Lists 5 cleanup components in order
Notes grace period is 20 seconds
Lists TeamDelete retry delays: 0s, 5s, 10s, 15s
Explains QUAL-012: gate filesystem fallback behind !cleanupTeamDeleteSucceeded
Describes CHOME pattern for multi-account support
Notes P1/P2 patterns persisted to Echoes
Mentions workflow lock release
Notes auto-mend option with --auto-mend flag

evals.prompt: What are all the flag interactions for /rune:appraise? Explain what happens with: --deep + --partial, --deep + --cycles N, --deep + --max-agents N, --deep + --no-converge, --cycles N standalone.
evals.expected_output: Flag interactions: --deep + --partial: Warning about sparse findings from investigation Ashes, proceeds (not hard error). --deep + --cycles N (N>1): Warning about N x 3 waves = up to {N*18} agent invocations, proceeds with cost warning. --deep + --max-agents N: Applies to Wave 1 only, Wave 2/3 are deepOnly and not subject to cap. --deep + --no-converge: Deep waves still execute, --no-converge affects per-chunk convergence not wave scheduling. --cycles N standalone: Run N standalone review passes with TOME merge between passes, no deep waves, useful for catching issues that require multiple passes.
expectations: Describes --deep + --partial: warning, proceeds
Describes --deep + --cycles N: warning about N*18 agents
Describes --deep + --max-agents: Wave 1 only
Describes --deep + --no-converge: affects chunk convergence, not waves
Describes --cycles N standalone: N passes with TOME merge
Notes all interactions are non-blocking warnings

evals.prompt: Explain the error handling matrix for /rune:appraise. What happens when: (1) Ash timeout (>5 min), (2) Total timeout (>10 min), (3) Ash crash, (4) ALL Ash fail, (5) Concurrent review running, (6) Codex CLI not installed, (7) Codex not authenticated.
evals.expected_output: Error handling: (1) Ash timeout (>5 min): proceed with partial results, log gap, report in TOME. (2) Total timeout (>10 min): final sweep, collect partial results, report incomplete. (3) Ash crash: report gap in TOME.md. (4) ALL Ash fail: abort, notify user. (5) Concurrent review: warn, offer to cancel previous workflow. (6) Codex CLI not installed: skip Codex Oracle, log 'CLI not found'. (7) Codex not authenticated: skip Codex Oracle, log 'run codex login'. (8) Codex exec timeout (>10 min): Codex Oracle partial results, log 'timeout — reduce context_budget'. (9) jq unavailable: Codex Oracle uses raw text fallback instead of JSONL parsing. All errors are non-blocking where possible.
expectations: Ash timeout: proceed with partial, log gap
Total timeout: final sweep, partial results, report incomplete
Ash crash: report gap in TOME.md
ALL Ash fail: abort, notify user
Concurrent review: warn, offer cancel
Codex not installed: skip Oracle, log message
Codex not authenticated: skip Oracle, suggest login
Codex timeout: partial results, suggest reduce budget
jq unavailable: raw text fallback

evals.prompt: How does Phase 0.5 Lore Layer work in /rune:appraise? When is it skipped? What does lore-analyst output? How does it affect file ordering?
evals.expected_output: Phase 0.5 Lore Layer: Purpose: git history risk scoring runs BEFORE team creation. lore-analyst agent (bare Agent, no team yet — ATE-1 exemption) outputs risk-map.json and lore-analysis.md. Risk tiers: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, STALE. File re-sorting: changed_files re-sorted by risk tier (CRITICAL → HIGH → MEDIUM → LOW → STALE). Skip conditions: non-git repo, --no-lore flag, talisman.goldmask.layers.lore.enabled === false, fewer than 5 commits in lookback window (G5 guard). The risk scores are used for prioritized review ordering and injected into inscription.json.
expectations: Notes lore-analyst runs as bare Agent (ATE-1 exemption)
Lists outputs: risk-map.json, lore-analysis.md
Lists risk tiers: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, STALE
Describes file re-sorting by risk tier
Lists skip conditions: non-git, --no-lore, lore.enabled === false, <5 commits (G5 guard)
Notes risk scores injected into inscription.json

evals.prompt: Explain Phase 0.3 Context Intelligence and Phase 0.4 Linter Detection for /rune:appraise. What metadata is gathered? How does linter awareness prevent duplicate findings?
evals.expected_output: Phase 0.3 Context Intelligence: Gathers PR metadata and linked issue context via gh CLI. Injects contextIntel into inscription.json. Includes sanitizeUntrustedText() for CDX-001/CVE-2021-42574 protection (Unicode bidi character stripping). Skipped when no gh CLI, --partial, or disabled in talisman. Phase 0.4 Linter Detection: Discovers project linters (eslint, prettier, ruff, clippy, etc.) via manifest scanning. Injects linter_context into inscription.json. Linter awareness suppresses duplicate findings — Ash skip reporting issues that linters would catch. SEC-*/VEIL-* findings are NEVER suppressed (security always reported). Configurable via talisman.review.linter_awareness.
expectations: Describes Phase 0.3: PR metadata, linked issues via gh CLI
Mentions sanitizeUntrustedText() for Unicode bidi protection
Notes skip conditions: no gh CLI, --partial, disabled
Describes Phase 0.4: linter discovery via manifest scanning
Lists example linters: eslint, prettier, ruff, clippy
Notes linter awareness suppresses duplicate findings
Mentions SEC-*/VEIL-* findings are NEVER suppressed
Notes configurable via talisman.review.linter_awareness

evals.prompt: How does the Workflow Lock work in /rune:appraise? What lock type is used? What happens when there's a conflict? How is the lock released?
evals.expected_output: Workflow Lock: Lock type: 'reader' (appraise reads repository state, doesn't modify files). Conflict detection: rune_check_conflicts('reader') checks for active writers (strive, mend, arc work phases). On conflict: AskUserQuestion prompts user to proceed anyway or wait. Lock acquisition: rune_acquire_lock('appraise', 'reader') creates lock file. Lock file location: tmp/.rune-lock-appraise-{identifier}.json. Lock release: Phase 7 cleanup releases lock via rm -f. Lock file includes: config_dir, owner_pid, session_id, workflow, lock_type, acquired_at. Session isolation: hooks verify owner_pid matches $PPID before acting on lock files.
expectations: States lock type is 'reader'
Notes conflict detection checks for active writers
Describes AskUserQuestion prompt on conflict
Names lock acquisition function: rune_acquire_lock
Notes lock release in Phase 7 cleanup
Lists lock file contents: config_dir, owner_pid, session_id, workflow, lock_type, acquired_at
Notes session isolation via owner_pid verification

evals.prompt: Explain the chunk routing logic for /rune:appraise. When does chunking trigger? What is CHUNK_THRESHOLD? How does the chunk lifecycle work?
evals.expected_output: Chunk routing: CHUNK_THRESHOLD = 20 files (default, configurable via --chunk-size N). Trigger: changed_files.length > CHUNK_THRESHOLD AND --no-chunk NOT set. Chunk lifecycle: (1) Split files into chunks of CHUNK_SIZE (default 20), (2) Each chunk runs full Roundtable Circle pass, (3) TOMEs merged between chunks, (4) Convergence loop runs after all chunks complete (unless --no-converge). Convergence: re-review files with P1/P2 findings from previous pass, up to MAX_CONVERGENCE_CYCLES. --no-chunk: forces single-pass review regardless of file count. --cycles N: alternative to chunking, runs N standalone passes with TOME merge.
expectations: States CHUNK_THRESHOLD = 20 files (default)
Notes configurable via --chunk-size N
Describes trigger: file count > threshold AND --no-chunk not set
Describes chunk lifecycle: split → pass per chunk → TOME merge → convergence
Mentions --no-chunk forces single-pass
Mentions --cycles N as alternative multi-pass approach
Notes convergence re-reviews P1/P2 files

evals.prompt: What is Phase 4.5 Doubt Seer in /rune:appraise? When does it run? What is the verdict format? How does BLOCK verdict affect the workflow?
evals.expected_output: Phase 4.5 Doubt Seer: CONDITIONAL — strict opt-in via talisman.doubt_seer.enabled = true. Purpose: cross-examines P1/P2 findings with adversarial questioning. Timeout: 5 minutes. Output: VERDICT field for each finding. Verdicts: PROVEN (high confidence), LIKELY (reasonable confidence), UNCERTAIN (needs investigation), UNPROVEN (evidence insufficient). BLOCK verdict: sets workflow_blocked flag in state file, prompts user to address issues before proceeding. Non-blocking: proceeds with findings, reports uncertainty. Spawned as bare Agent (ATE-1 exemption) for cross-examination. Uses categorical verdicts for structured output.
expectations: Notes strict opt-in: talisman.doubt_seer.enabled = true
States purpose: cross-examine P1/P2 findings
Notes 5 minute timeout
Lists verdicts: PROVEN, LIKELY, UNCERTAIN, UNPROVEN
Describes BLOCK verdict: sets workflow_blocked flag
Notes spawned as bare Agent (ATE-1 exemption)
Mentions categorical verdicts for structured output

evals.prompt: Explain the --dry-run flag behavior for /rune:appraise. What phases execute? What is displayed? What is NOT created?
evals.expected_output: --dry-run behavior: Executes Phase 0 (Pre-flight) and Phase 1 (Rune Gaze) only. Displays: changed files classified by type, which Ash would be summoned, file assignments per Ash, estimated team size, chunk plan if file count exceeds CHUNK_THRESHOLD, warnings about sparse findings (if --deep + --partial). Does NOT create: teams (TeamCreate), tasks (TaskCreate), state files, inscription.json, signal directories, agents (Agent tool). Exits after display with summary. Useful for previewing review scope before committing to full execution.
expectations: States executes Phase 0 and Phase 1 only
Lists displayed info: files, Ash plan, assignments, team size, chunk plan
Notes --deep + --partial warning shown
Lists NOT created: teams, tasks, state files, inscription.json, signals, agents
Notes exits after display with summary
Describes use case: preview scope before full execution
